_comment: A larger hand-placed cozy world: a central clearing that opens onto a berry grove to the north, a flowered meadow and stone basin to the southwest, a lantern-lit rest spot to the east, and a crystal hollow with a second pond to the northeast. Coordinates are world-space pixels. Colors are [r,g,b] 0..1. world_art draws it; world_controller uses 'interactables' for proximity. Each interactable's 'type' picks how it's drawn (see world_art). Edit freely to reshape the place.
_regions_comment: Named AREAS for new-area discovery (a bond source). The companion's bond deepens the first time it reaches each region (novelty-gated, so re-entering pays nothing). An area's id is world_id:region_id; a position in no region is just the world_id (the open 'wilds'). Because ids are world-namespaced and the companion remembers across sessions/worlds, this scales from sub-regions of one big world to entirely separate worlds. Rectangles in world space; first match wins; gaps fall through to the world default area.
_border_comment: A procedural, jittered treeline just inside the bounds so the world feels enclosed rather than ending at the void. Presentation-only (world_art builds it); seeded, so it's identical every run.
_collision_comment: Barriers. tree_radius/great_tree_radius are the blocking circles for trees and landmark great-trees; pond_blocks makes water solid; body_radius is how big the player/companion are for collision; margin stops them a touch short of things. Tall props (bench, signpost, lantern, crystal, log, berry_bush) block by default — any interactable can override with its own "solid": true/false and "collision_radius".
_landmarks_comment: Oversized, beckoning features (a great tree) you can spot from afar — anchors that pull you to explore. Presentation-only; drawn by world_art._draw_landmark.
_atmosphere_comment: Presentation-only mood knobs — none of this touches game logic. Tune freely and 'go play it'. day_tint is a global warm color-wash (CanvasModulate); vignette darkens the screen edges; wind drives the gentle sway of flowers/canopies/grass; ground_noise mottles the flat ground; pollen is the count of drifting motes that follow the camera; glow.pulse_speed breathes the lantern/crystal light.
_interactables_comment: Each has a stable 'id' — the companion keys its habituation/memory off it, so editing a prop's position or label keeps its identity (and the bond it earned). Keep ids unique and stable across edits. 'tags' are NEUTRAL descriptors of the thing; the companion's appraisal (see companion.json 'appraisal') decides how it feels about them — author tags freely without thinking about psychology.
_portals_comment: Doorways to other worlds — walk through one to travel. world_controller renders them (via world_art's 'portal' type) and watches for the player stepping into them; WorldRouter performs the scene swap and the arriving world sets you down beside its matching 'target_portal'. This one opens onto the Riverbank.
